title: Modified pH drop protocol (K+)
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"9de3u","text":"Prepare the K+electrode for use","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3vfm9","text":"Do slope check for electrode performance","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ahapl","text":"Standard curve","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"91huv","text":"Modified pH drop protocol","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"mm5r","text":"Here are some notes from my notebook from 2002:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a1qiq","text":"When using the K+ electrode, need to read mV for the standard curve.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":16,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7kujb","text":"Also need to do ALL readings in a 0.1M NaCl background.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"83n34","text":"These electrodes only have a 6-month life span.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"177r9","text":"K+ electrodes are made from the following solutions:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":1,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"340ui","text":"1M KCl to dilute for standard curve","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b5o19","text":"1M NaCl to make the 0.1M background solution","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7i0ed","text":"1mM MgCl2 as the “neutral salts” solution for the modified pH drop protocol (noKCl)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":8,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5dskf","text":"0.5M NaOH to pH the cell suspension to 7.2 for the modified pH drop protocol (noKCl)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"eg69l","text":"0.1M KCl to add to cells with glucose for modified protocol","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Remove the rubber caps.
Put 0.1M NaCl ina beaker. Stir. Put the meter at the mV setting.
0mM (no K + ) = 9mL water + 1mL 1M NaCl
Read pH & mV readings atdesired intervals.
Fill with 0.1M NaCl filling solution provided to just below the hole in the probe.
Gently shake the probe like a thermometer to remove all air bubbles.
Immerse the electrode in 0.1M KCl for 20-30 minutes.
Rinse off.
Lower the K+electrode into the beaker.
Add 1mL 0.1M KCl to the beaker & keep stirring. Once the reading is stable, record the mV reading (mine was-49.2).
Add 10mL 0.1M KCl to the beaker. Continue stirring. Record mV reading (mine was6.1).
The difference between the readings should be 52mV ± 2. (Mine was 55.3mV – called that good enough!)
Take 1M stock KCl & dilute 1:10 in 8mL water + 1mL 1M NaCl to create the following K + concentrations: 0.1M (10-1) 0.01M (10-2) 1mM (10-3) 0.1mM (10-4) 0.01mM (10-5), 1µM (10-6)
Read mV for each standard. Create standard curve: electrode potential (mV) vs. K+concentration (mM)
Prepare 100mL ON of culture as per pH drop protocol (in BHI + 1% glucose).
Measure OD 700 .
Pellet cells.
Wash cell pellet 2X in 30-35mL 1mM MgCl 2 .
Resuspend pellet in x mL 1mM MgCl 2 , where x = # from formula
For each experiment, take 4.5 mL cells + 0.5mL 1M NaCl → 0.1M NaCl in the final 5mL cell suspension.
Adjust pH to 7.2 with 0.5M NaOH.
Add 100µL 50% glucose = time zero.
creator: Eadewunm
